---
title: app.runtime.yaml
description: The runtime-owned contract for a workspace app — lifecycle, MCP, health checks, and integration declarations.
---

`app.runtime.yaml` is the runtime-owned contract for a workspace app.

The source of truth is:

- `runtime/api-server/src/workspace-apps.ts`
- `runtime/api-server/src/workspace-runtime-plan.ts`
- `runtime/api-server/src/integration-types.ts`
- `runtime/api-server/src/app-lifecycle-worker.ts`

## Minimal Working Example

The runtime resolves the manifest from `workspace.yaml`:

```yaml title="workspace.yaml"
applications:
  - app_id: my_app
    config_path: apps/my_app/app.runtime.yaml
```

Then it parses `apps/my_app/app.runtime.yaml`:

```yaml title="apps/my_app/app.runtime.yaml"
app_id: "my_app"
name: "My App"
slug: "my-app"

lifecycle:
  setup: "npm install"
  start: "npm run start"
  stop: "pkill -f my-app || true"

healthchecks:
  mcp:
    path: /mcp/health
    timeout_s: 30
    interval_s: 5

mcp:
  transport: http-sse
  port: 13100
  path: /mcp/sse
  tools:
    - create_record

env_contract:
  - HOLABOSS_WORKSPACE_ID

integrations:
  - key: primary_google
    provider: google
    capability: gmail
    scopes:
      - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.modify
    required: true
    credential_source: platform
    holaboss_user_id_required: true
```

## Field Guide

| Field | Current runtime behavior |
| --- | --- |
| `app_id` | Must exactly match the `workspace.yaml` app entry |
| `name`, `slug` | Metadata only for the current runtime |
| `lifecycle.setup` | Optional setup command run before start |
| `lifecycle.start` | Primary native shell lifecycle entrypoint |
| `lifecycle.stop` | Stop command for the shell lifecycle path |
| top-level `start` | Fallback subprocess lifecycle when shell lifecycle is not declared |
| `mcp.transport` | Defaults to `http-sse` |
| `mcp.port` | Required |
| `mcp.path` | Defaults vary across runtime helpers; keep it explicit |
| `mcp.tools` | Optional raw tool names used for MCP registry reconciliation |
| `healthchecks.mcp.path` | Preferred MCP health path |
| `healthchecks.*.timeout_s` | Defaults to `60` |
| `healthchecks.*.interval_s` | Defaults to `5` |
| `env_contract` | String list used for selected lifecycle env injection |
| `integrations` | Preferred multi-integration form |
| legacy `integration` | Still supported, but cannot be combined with `integrations` |

## Hard Requirements

### `app_id` Must Match the Workspace Entry

If `workspace.yaml` contains:

```yaml title="workspace.yaml"
applications:
  - app_id: twitter
    config_path: apps/twitter/app.runtime.yaml
```

then the manifest must also contain:

```yaml title="apps/twitter/app.runtime.yaml"
app_id: "twitter"
```

The runtime rejects mismatches.

### `mcp.port` Is Required

`parseResolvedAppRuntime()` errors if `mcp.port` is missing or not numeric. Today the runtime expects every managed app manifest to declare an MCP block and port.

### Only One Lifecycle Mode Should Win

The runtime supports three launch modes:

1. Docker Compose when no native lifecycle is declared and a compose file exists
2. native shell lifecycle from `lifecycle.start`
3. native subprocess lifecycle from top-level `start`

Do not build a manifest that leaves the runtime guessing between them.

## Health Check Resolution

`workspace-apps.ts` resolves the preferred health check in this order:

1. `healthchecks.mcp`
2. `healthchecks.api`
3. the first object value under `healthchecks`

If nothing is declared, the runtime falls back to:

- path: `/health`
- timeout: `60`
- interval: `5`

Make `healthchecks.mcp.path` explicit and keep it aligned with the server code.

## `env_contract` Behavior

`env_contract` is narrower than many app specs imply.

In the current lifecycle executor it is only used to decide whether to inject selected runtime variables such as:

- `HOLABOSS_USER_ID`
- `HOLABOSS_WORKSPACE_ID` when that variable has not already been injected through integration resolution

It does not magically expose arbitrary host environment variables.

## Integration Declarations

The parser accepts:

```yaml title="app.runtime.yaml"
integrations:
  - key: primary_google
    provider: google
    capability: gmail
    scopes:
      - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.modify
    required: true
    credential_source: platform
    holaboss_user_id_required: true
```

Current `credential_source` values are:

- `platform`
- `manual`
- `broker`

Do not declare both `integration` and `integrations` in the same file.

## MCP Registry Side Effects

If the manifest declares:

```yaml title="app.runtime.yaml"
mcp:
  tools:
    - create_post
    - list_posts
```

the runtime can rewrite `workspace.yaml` so the allowlist contains:

- `my_app.create_post`
- `my_app.list_posts`

and `mcp_registry.servers.my_app` points at the app's runtime MCP URL.

## Practical Rules

- keep `mcp.path` explicit instead of relying on helper defaults
- keep `healthchecks.mcp.path` explicit and tested
- make `lifecycle.setup` safe to rerun
- do not assume manifest ports are the runtime ports; use injected `PORT` and `MCP_PORT`
- prefer `integrations` over legacy `integration`

## Validation

```bash
npm run runtime:api-server:test
npm run runtime:test
```
